title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
summary: Kimi Code is a video reasoning tool built on Kimi K2.6 that accepts reference videos and generates .cube LUT (color lookup table) files based on visual queries about colors, shots, or visual style. Users can drag in video references and request specific visual attributes to generate ready-to-use color grading outputs.
writeup: The video demonstrates Kimi Code, a video reasoning tool built on Kimi K2.6 that takes reference videos and produces .cube LUT (color lookup table) files in response to visual queries about colors, shots, or visual style. The workflow lets users drag in video references and request specific visual attributes, returning ready-to-use color grading outputs. The takeaway is a multimodal pipeline that converts visual analysis of footage directly into applicable color grading files.
